mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS fail to associate with positive 20-day SPX returns (two-sided t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: After the trigger fires, positive 60-day SPX returns occur in more than 45% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts and the federal funds rate capture a demand collapse that monetary easing is insufficient to offset, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of declining HOUST and declining FEDFUNDS does not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5 percent, month-over-month declines in retail sales signal collapsing consumer confidence and forced institutional de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.0%, month-over-month RSAFS declines do not coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO fail to associate with positive 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 2.5 percent, emergency monetary easing signals severe systemic stress that triggers flight-to-safety behavior, overwhelming the discount-rate channel and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS < 2.5%, subsequent 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening household income and aggregate demand, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS changes and 5-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices concurrent with falling industrial production generate a stagflationary profits squeeze where firms cannot pass through costs amid weakening demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and falling INDPRO does not coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.
